archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

evidence.detail: Root-level CI pipeline orchestration script — entry point that runs analysis and PRD health validation
Root-level orchestration script integrating Claude CLI; sits at the orchestration tier alongside cli.js and web.js
Root-level help display script at the orchestration tier; no package sub-context
Adaptive analysis logic for the agent loop — business logic orchestration within the agent/analysis sub-zone
Review analysis module — agent decision logic, not a UI or data model
Spin-detection analysis — runtime analysis service within the agent loop
Stuck-detection analysis — runtime analysis service within the agent loop
Summary generation for agent runs — business logic service
Workflow analysis for the agent — orchestration logic within analysis sub-zone
Agent completion handling — business logic for determining and recording task completion
CLI-mode agent loop management — lifecycle orchestration service
Heartbeat mechanism for long-running agent process — lifecycle service
Core agent loop — primary lifecycle orchestration, high fan-in from lifecycle sub-zone
Shared utilities and helpers across lifecycle modules — infrastructure support, not primary logic
Task display formatting helper — presentation utility within lifecycle sub-zone
Token budget management — stateful business logic service tracking and enforcing token limits
Token usage tracking and reporting — service layer for usage analytics within the agent
Task brief construction — orchestrates context gathering and brief generation per CLAUDE.md brief/ sub-zone description
Prompt construction for agent tasks — service that builds LLM prompts from brief/context
Tool dispatch and implementations — core agent service managing tool-use loop
CLI help command handler within the hench CLI sub-directory
Guard command validation logic — runtime enforcement service for command safety
Guard contract type definitions — defines interfaces and type shapes for the guard system, no logic
Path guard utilities — shared helper for path validation/filtering within the guard sub-zone
Policy enforcement for the guard system — business logic that evaluates and applies safety rules
Explicit gateway per CLAUDE.md: concentrates all hench→@n-dx/llm-client imports, re-exports only
Explicit gateway per CLAUDE.md: concentrates all hench→rex imports, re-exports only
Metrics collection and tracking for concurrent process execution — stateful service within process sub-zone
Shell execution wrapper — shared infrastructure utility for running shell commands
Process execution utility — low-level infrastructure helper, high fan-in expected from process sub-zone consumers
Process lifecycle orchestration — orphan detection, stale-run cleanup, resource monitoring logic
Cross-process concurrency control using PID-based lock files — stateful infrastructure service
Cross-platform system memory reading utility — pure helper with no stateful behavior
Memory-based execution gate with threshold decision logic — stateful throttling service
Runtime process pool managing warm worker acquisition/release — stateful resource pool
Per-process RSS sampling with ring buffers and threshold alerting — stateful monitoring service
FIFO execution queue with priority override — stateful queue management service
Pure formatting helpers for queue status CLI display — no state, high reuse potential
Priority resolution engine bridging PRD metadata and ExecutionQueue — orchestration logic
Defines ToolGuard interface and ToolContext — pure type/contract definitions, no logic
Boundary module concentrating cross-zone tool definitions (TOOL_DEFINITIONS) between agent-core and tools zones
Shared shell execution wrapper reused by multiple tool implementations — pure infrastructure helper
File tool implementations (read/write/list/search) with guards and business logic
Git tool implementation with subcommand guarding and execution delegation
Rex integration tool — validates completion and dispatches PRD status updates
Shell command tool with guard layer and delegation to execShell
Post-task test runner that locates co-located test files and executes project test command
Task completion validation via git diff and test execution — business logic service
Direct Anthropic SDK API client with retry/backoff logic — external API service
Authentication detection helpers (CLI availability, API key validation) — stateless utilities
Claude CLI binary spawning with JSON/stream parsing — alternative LLM provider service
Codex vendor provider implementing the same LLM client contract via codex exec
Factory with provider auto-selection logic (API key vs CLI fallback) — client construction service
Centralized process execution abstraction used across all packages — high fan-in utility
ANSI color formatting helpers for CLI output — pure stateless utilities
Canonical JSON serialization utility (toCanonicalJSON) — pure helper
Vendor-neutral LLM client factory with createLLMClient and detectAuthMode — top-level service facade
Configuration loader for .n-dx.json with vendor settings extraction and deep-merge
Vendor-neutral type definitions (LLMVendor, CodexConfig, LLMConfig) — pure type contracts
Shared config composition utilities (deepMerge) used across packages — stateless helper
Pure constants file — exports only PROJECT_DIRS object and ProjectDir type with no logic.
Pure interface and type definitions — LLMProvider, ProviderInfo, StreamChunk, ProviderAuthMode, ProviderCapability — no logic.
ProviderRegistry class manages factory registrations and config-driven provider creation; business logic orchestration for vendor selection.
ProviderSession class maintains active provider state, handles vendor switching and config updates — stateful service layer.
Shared Levenshtein-based typo correction utilities (editDistance, suggestCommands, formatTypoSuggestion) used across CLI packages.
Parsing utilities for token usage from API/CLI/stream responses — pure transformation functions with high reuse.
Acknowledged findings store with I/O (loadAcknowledged, saveAcknowledged), hash computation, and pure mutation helpers — data access + business logic.
Shared constants and types (DEFAULT_MODEL, ClaudeResult, etc.) consumed across the analyze pipeline — companion file, no logic.
Pure type definitions for BatchAcceptanceRecord and GranularityAdjustmentRecord — no logic, no I/O.
Post-processing LLM consolidation pass with applyConsolidationGuard — orchestrates prompt building, LLM calls, and result evaluation.
Decomposition pipeline for breaking down proposals via LLM — business logic orchestration.
Deduplication logic for proposals — utility transformation with no side effects.
Diff computation between proposal sets — pure transformation utility.
Extraction utilities for parsing LLM responses into structured data — shared helper functions.
File validation utilities for the analyze pipeline — input guards with no domain state.
Guided interactive analysis flow — orchestrates user prompts, LLM calls, and proposal iteration.
Module-level LLM client singleton bridge — manages configuration, lazy client creation, and spawnClaude dispatch.
LLM-powered proposal modification pipeline — orchestrates prompt building, retries, validation, and result assembly.
Proposal creation and assembly logic — domain service that builds structured proposals from analysis output.
Core LLM reasoning engine — prompt construction, spawnClaude calls, response parsing, and quality validation.
Reconciliation of new proposals against existing PRD items — domain service with business logic.
LLM-powered reshape reasoning for PRD reorganization suggestions — orchestrates prompt and LLM interaction.
Project file scanners that gather context for analysis — data fetching and aggregation service.
Validates modification requests before LLM calls — pure guard utility with no side effects.
CLI help text strings and formatting helpers — shared utility for CLI command output.
MCP tool handler implementations — each exported function handles an inbound tool call request and returns a structured MCP response, mirroring HTTP route handler pattern.
MCP server setup — registers tools, configures transport, and wires tool handlers to the server instance.
Input validation utilities for CLI argument parsing — pure guard functions shared across CLI commands.
Detects conflicts between recommendations and existing PRD items — pure analysis utility.
Creates PRD items from sourcevision recommendations — domain service orchestrating item construction and PRD mutation.
Exports a single DEFAULT_WORKFLOW string constant — pure data, no logic.
Defines BUILTIN_ARCHETYPES catalog — structured constant data with signal patterns, no runtime logic.
Classifies PRD items by change significance using heuristic logic — business logic orchestration.
Collects completed PRD items from git diff and .rex/prd.json — data fetching and orchestration.
Pure filtering logic for branch work items by timestamp — stateless helper functions.
Manages persistent JSON storage for branch work records with read/write state management.
Generates architectural findings from call graph data — analysis and business logic.
TypeScript compiler API-based call graph extraction — stateful analysis pipeline.
File classification engine accumulating weighted signal scores with optional LLM enrichment.
Vendor-aware LLM API client wrapper — API client and configuration management.
Reads PRD completion state from disk with graceful degradation — data fetching layer.
Single-pass React component and route analyzer using TypeScript compiler API.
Generates CONTEXT.md output — orchestrates structured output generation.
Tracks zone metrics across runs and produces delta comparison reports.
Batch processing orchestrator for AI enrichment with retry logic and prompt construction.
Configuration constants and helpers for AI enrichment passes — pure config data.
JSON parsing and finding extraction utilities — stateless helper functions.
Per-zone AI enrichment orchestrator with parallelization — business logic and coordination.

File inventory analyzer detecting language, role, and line counts — analysis pipeline stage.
Generates llms.txt structured output — orchestrates multi-section document generation.

Reads and writes manifest.json with git metadata — data access and persistence layer.
File-move recommendation engine detecting divergence and import majority — analysis and recommendation logic.
Derives prioritized next steps from findings — business logic orchestration.
Architectural risk scoring for zones with cohesion/coupling thresholds — domain logic.

Generates per-zone output files (context.md, summary.json) — orchestration logic

Sits in the WebSocket message pipeline as a gate/filter — suspends and resumes message flow based on tab visibility, a classic middleware pattern.
RAF-based batching utility for scheduling UI state updates; standalone infrastructure module with zero framework dependencies.
Infrastructure dispatcher that bridges tick-timer to useTick hook consumers via batched RAF updates; standalone utility with no framework coupling.
Centralized registry and lifecycle manager for polling intervals — orchestrates suspend/resume across the app, fitting the service layer pattern.
Thin coordinator that bridges graceful-degradation system to polling-state; single-purpose glue module with no framework dependencies.
Central registry/store for all polling sources with suspend/resume/dispose state management — high fan-in, shared mutable state, store pattern.
Singleton wrapper around the Page Visibility API providing a shared source of truth; infrastructure utility with subscriber pattern.
Shared tick timer service managing a single setInterval and distributing tick events to subscribers — fits the service layer pattern.
Pipeline bridge/gate that suspends the tick timer based on tab visibility — classic middleware position between tab-visibility and tick-timer.
Schema version migration utility — transforms older data formats for newer viewers; pure logic module with no framework dependencies.
Shared path display helpers with re-exports for backward compatibility; high fan-in utility module.
Zone color palette constants and lookup helpers — primarily constants/data with minimal logic, single source of truth for color tokens.
Flow-diagram data transformation functions converting zone/call data into node/edge arrays; pure data-transformation utility.
Metric classification helpers (meterClass) for visualization components; small focused utility module.
CI/PR gate pipeline script that spawns build and validation steps; orchestration utility at the monorepo root.
Sourcevision dashboard artifact bundler/writer; infrastructure utility for the refresh pipeline.
Refresh flag validation and execution plan builder; pure logic utility supporting the refresh orchestration flow.
Snapshot capture/rollback utility for sourcevision output files during refresh; infrastructure helper.
Top-level server orchestration entry point for ndx start/web commands — manages PID, port, daemon mode, and process lifecycle.
